title: viscum album var. pini
intro: Viscum album var. pini, also known as European mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species, particularly in the oak and pine family. Traditionally, the leaves and berries of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits associated with Viscum album var. pini include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of chronic diseases such as cancer and multiple sclerosis. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance their effects.
